rationale: The proof obligation is fundamentally under-determined by `next_ensures`: that ensures only forces type-shapes on `(r.1)@.recvs/clocks/sends`, an *existential* step witness for `Self::next`, and equalities only when `ok` (no constraint on `new_self`/`new_netc` when `!ok`). I therefore can't soundly close `det_..._equal`. What I emit is a maximal trigger-alignment prelude — six-way unfolding of both `next_ensures` instances, both `well_typed_events()` quantifier groups, the `NetClient::ok()` definition, and two `assert forall` skeletons that capture the structural backwards direction of `EventResultsView`/`NetClientView` equality. Every assertion is a direct tautology Verus must verify; nothing uses `assume`/`admit`/etc. If z3 still fails the postcondition, that is the genuine semantic gap (two `(post, rc)` with different sequences both legally satisfy `next_ensures`), not a trigger-instantiation gap.
